title: What Am I Postponing Until Conditions Are Perfect?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for the things you keep waiting to start
excerpt: A set of five journal prompts for examining what you keep waiting to begin. The prompts move from naming the postponed thing toward identifying what good enough conditions would actually look like.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice you have been talking about starting something for longer than you have been working on it.

content.text: A journal prompt set for the things that stay in the someday list while the conditions are not quite right. The prompts ask what you are waiting for and whether the waiting is serving the thing or the fear of the thing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What is the thing I keep not starting?
Name it specifically. Not "my health" or "my finances." The concrete thing: the conversation, the course, the application, the project.
What conditions am I waiting for before I begin?
Write them down without editing. More money, more time, a different season of life, a different body, a better version of the circumstances.
How long have I been waiting for those conditions?
Write the actual number. Not how long you have been interested in the thing, but how long you have been actively waiting for the right moment to arrive.
Are those conditions likely to arrive, or am I waiting for something that keeps moving?
Some conditions are real constraints that will resolve. Others are the horizon: they move as you approach them. Write which this is.
What would a small enough version of this look like to begin today?
Not the full thing. The smallest version of a first move that uses the time and resources available right now, not the ones you are waiting for.
